content: Comparative analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be reframed as a study of how a meta-agent selects and prunes its own active memory modules to adapt to sparse-reward environments, rather than just a comparison of separate curiosity methods for generic agents. This reframing turns existing benchmark comparisons into a testbed for how different intrinsic reward signals shape an agent's long-term active memory organization.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
